decision.archive_summary: The session is working on Codex context minor GC regressions. Earlier resolved work added broader guarded-relation handling for `continue` and added a fixed live eval case for a current-editor detour. A harder Chinese multi-topic return case was also added to fixed live evals. Durable intent to retain: avoid whitelist-style hardcoding; prefer generalizable guard logic.
decision.reasoning_summary: The latest user turn is a same-topic continuation request, so relation is continue. The next turn still depends on the immediately recent raw context around the concern about whether the previously failing tests now pass and that the assistant already ran an automated eval, so those recent turns should stay raw. Older solved status updates about earlier added cases can leave the prompt. The anti-whitelist preference and resulting guard-shape decision are still relevant and worth preserving as compact pins even if older raw turns are trimmed.
